mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held above CPIAUCSL inflation and does not fall, markets infer restrictive policy will persist, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next month through higher expected short rates and term premium.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL inflation trend by at least 1.0pp
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains above inflation but the 10-year yield falls on average over the next 20 trading days in a majority of triggered cases, or if the sign of the yield change is negative more than 60% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor-market weakening and softer growth expectations, which lowers expected policy rates and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months but the 10-year yield increases over the following 20 trading days in more than 55% of triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in RSAFS reflects collapsing nominal demand, reducing inflation pressure and growth expectations, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls within the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls year-over-year by at least 3%
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS declines by at least 3% y/y but the 10-year yield rises over the next 20 trading days in most triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized drop in INDPRO and HOUST indicates broad real-activity contraction, which lowers inflation expectations and term premium, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly declines in INDPRO and HOUST are followed by higher 10-year yields in a majority of 20-day forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a severe labor shock, falling PAYEMS together with rising UNRATE signals recessionary stress; this drives a flight to duration and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS decline / UNRATE increase pattern is followed by a positive 5-day yield change in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below it, investors expect policy to catch up; that repricing lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes are not followed by higher 10-year yields in at least 55% of cases over the next 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, strengthening GDPC1 growth and rising PAYEMS restore growth optimism and reduce recession hedging, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth turns positive and PAYEMS rises for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP growth plus consecutive payroll gains are not followed by higher 10-year yields over 60 trading days in most recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: If INDPRO is falling while HOUST is simultaneously weak, the economy is losing production and construction momentum; that broad supply-side deterioration lowers inflation expectations and drives the 10-year Treasury yield down over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent weakness in industrial production and housing starts is followed by a positive 60-day yield change in more than half of observed cases.
